pi_items.verified_value: Oscar EMR (Open Source Clinical Application Resource)
pi_items.source: Web search confirms Oscar EMR is a real, widely-deployed open-source clinical application developed at McMaster University, written in Java/JSP with MySQL backend. Docker image used: openosp/open-osp:release (MariaDB 10.5)
task.json description and setup_oscar.sh line 217 confirms account configuration for user 'oscardoc'
task.json description and setup_oscar.sh line 217 (SHA-1 hash confirms password 'oscar' is set)
task.json description and setup_oscar.sh line 217 references PIN 1117
setup_task.sh comments on line 3 state 'Seeds the database with specific patients'. Patient data is not from any external source but generated by the setup script for testing purposes.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'export_patient_cohort@1' uses Oscar EMR (a real open-source clinical system from McMaster University) with synthetic test data. Target patients are Lucas Target (born 2020-03-15) and Sophia Target (born 2020-11-20), both within the required 2020 date range. Distractor patients are Liam Older (2019-12-31, before 2020) and Olivia Younger (2021-01-01, after 2020), designed to test proper date filtering. The task requires exporting matching patients to /home/ga/Documents/2020_cohort.csv using login credentials (oscardoc/oscar/PIN:1117). All metadata claims about patient names, dates of birth, and system credentials are verified as correct.
